Section Title: Silver ETFs > Frequently Asked Questions > What are the costs associated with Silver ETFs?
Content:
Investors should consider the expense ratio, which includes storage and insurance costs if the ETF holds physical silver. Brokerage fees apply when buying or selling ETF units. However, Dhan offers free delivery for ETFs, which can help reduce transaction costs significantly.

Section Title: Best Silver ETFs to Invest in India – Top Picks & Comparison > How Silver ETFs Work
Content:
Expense ratio: The annual fee charged by the fund to manage the ETF. This is deducted over time and slightly impacts returns.
Tracking error: The difference between the ETF’s performance and actual silver price movement. Lower tracking error usually means closer price matching.
 ...

Section Title: ... > **Silver ETF Expense Ratio Comparison** — Which Costs the Least?
Content:
The silver ETF expense ratio comparison is one of the most important factors for long-term investors. Since all these funds track the same silver price, a lower expense ratio directly translates to better net returns over time.
What to know about expense ratios for the best silver ETFs india 2026:
Most Indian Silver ETFs charge between 0.25%–0.65% per annum
Over a 5–10 year holding period, even a 0.20% difference in expense ratio can meaningfully impact compounded returns
When comparing top 10 best silver ETF in India options, always check the latest expense ratio on the AMC website or AMFI portal — ratios can change after initial launch
**Pro Tip** : In any silver ETF expense ratio comparison, don’t just look at the stated TER (Total Expense Ratio) — also check the actual silver ETF tracking error comparison data, as a low-cost ETF with high tracking error may underperform a slightly costlier but more accurate fund.
Section Title: Best Silver ETFs to Invest in India – Top Picks & Comparison > How to Choose the Right Silver ETF
Content:
Not all Silver ETFs work the same way. Small differences can affect your returns over time, so it helps to be selective.
Here’s what to look at before choosing one:
Expense ratio: Lower costs matter, especially if you plan to hold the ETF for years.
Tracking difference: This shows how closely the ETF follows silver prices. Smaller gaps are better.
Liquidity: Check daily trading volume—higher liquidity makes buying and selling easier.
Fund size (AUM): Larger funds are usually more stable and easier to trade.

Commodity ETFs may not perfectly match the underlying commodity’s price due to expenses, market liquidity, or rolling costs in the case of commodity derivatives. This creates small gaps between ETF returns and actual commodity performance.
 ... 
Section Title: List of Best Commodities ETFs in India 2026 > ... > Expense Ratio and Rolling Costs
Content:
Gold and silver ETFs charge annual fees for managing, storing, and insuring bullion. For any ETF that uses derivatives (rare in India), rolling futures contracts may add additional costs. Higher expenses over time reduce the ETF’s ability to match the commodity’s performance.

Section Title: Silver ETF > **How Do** **Silver ETF** **s Work?**
Content:
Now, suppose you purchase 100 units of a silver ETF at ₹100 each by paying ₹10,000. In this case, you would
essentially own 100 grams worth of silver. If the price of the precious metal rises to ₹150 per gram, the ETF's
value will also increase proportionately. At this point, you may choose to liquidate your holdings by selling the
units on the exchanges at a profit of ₹5,000 (₹50 per unit x 100 units).
Unlike equity or debt mutual funds, silver ETFs are passive investment options that require no active monitoring.
Therefore, these funds usually have much lower expense ratios, making them highly cost-efficient investment
options.
 ...

results.title: 5 Leading Commodities Mutual Funds in India - Views on News from Equitymaster
excerpts:
Section Title: 5 Leading Commodities Mutual Funds in India > \ SBI Gold Fund > \ Axis Silver FoF
Content:
The [Axis Silver FoF](https://www.personalfn.com/mutual-funds/best-funds/factsheet/axis-silver-fof-reg-g?utm_source=the-fundstrategist&utm_medium=website&utm_campaign=e-letter&utm_content=the-fundstrategist) tracks the returns of the Axis Silver ETF. Given the physical nature of silver and its bulkiness, the FoF format offers convenience as an investment vehicle.
Silver demand is driven by fast-growing industrial uses (solar, electronics, electric vehicle supply chains) and rising investment interest.
The fund states that including silver in the portfolio reduces risk due to its lower correlation with other assets. However, note that silver can be more volatile than equities, with outperformance in times of uncertainty, followed by a crash.
Section Title: 5 Leading Commodities Mutual Funds in India > \ SBI Gold Fund > \ Axis Silver FoF
Content:
This is why silver, as an asset class, works best as a tactical or satellite allocation rather than a core holding, requiring disciplined position sizing and a clear investment horizon.
The fund invests at least 95% in units of the Axis Silver ETF, with the allocation potentially rising to 100% during favorable market cycles. The remaining AUM is invested in debt and money market instruments.
The portfolio shows 100.64% exposure to the Axis Silver ETF, offset by -0.64% in debt, cash, and other current assets.
This reflects near-pure silver exposure, making returns largely dependent on silver price movements rather than on active asset allocation decisions.
